paper_title: A New Map of Pleistocene Proglacial Lake Tight Based on GIS Modeling and Analysis

methods.description: This method involves acquiring and preparing Digital Elevation Models (DEMs) from the USGS National Elevation Dataset to model the Lake Tight area.

steps.description: One-arc-second DEMs were downloaded through The National Map on the USGS website.
steps.searchable_summary: download 1-arc-second DEMs from USGS National Map

steps.description: Eleven DEM coverages of areas of Ohio, West Virginia, and Kentucky were added to the GIS.
The DEMs were combined into one DEM.
steps.searchable_summary: merge multiple DEMs into a single DEM

methods.description: This method defines contours and boundaries for Lake Tight using GIS and geomorphological considerations.

steps.description: The GIS created a 25-meter contour interval feature class from the DEMs, including a 275-meter limiting contour for Lake Tight.
steps.searchable_summary: generate contour feature class from DEMs

steps.description: The 275-meter contour and the boundaries developed for the glacial ice margin, Muskingum River area, and western boundary were combined into a single linear feature class.
steps.searchable_summary: combine contour and boundary features into a linear feature class

steps.description: This linear feature class was converted into a seamless polygon feature: the lake polygon.
steps.searchable_summary: convert linear feature class to polygon feature

methods.description: This method identifies potential islands within Lake Tight and calculates the area of the lake excluding these islands.

steps.description: Island polygons that contained at least 1 DEM point that was ≥277.5 meters were considered an island.
steps.searchable_summary: identify island polygons based on DEM point elevation

steps.description: A spatial location query captured all islands that did not have a DEM 277.5-point within them but were of an area comparable to or greater than that of the smallest island extracted by the DEM point method.
steps.searchable_summary: perform spatial query to identify additional islands

steps.description: The cumulative area of the island polygons was subtracted from the area of the lake polygon to determine the area of Lake Tight.
